workspaceContextPolicies.rationale: Machine-global installation diagnosis must run before a Workspace can be resolved.
Onboarding must inspect an explicitly selected legacy project before Workspace authority exists.
Workspace creation preview starts from an explicit config before the target Workspace exists.
Authorized Workspace creation applies an explicit preview before the target Workspace exists.

positive: build finished, generate CHANGELOG.md from Done backlog stories
after deploy, sync user-facing changelog with acceptance evidence
negative: design new stories for a feature backlog
fix a failing unit test through TCR

positive: the fly-mode build request is vague and needs scope questions
the user asked for a feature but boundaries are underspecified
Workspace requirement discovery returned a closed workspace_target handoff and the agent must ask whether to select an existing Workspace or start a create preview
Workspace discovery is incomplete and the closed workspace_target handoff allows only canonical repair actions
negative: the user supplied a concrete US-XXX story id
the user only wants intent restated, not scope questions
silently select the only active Workspace even though it does not match the current requirement
apply or activate a Workspace after a workspace_target create or select answer

positive: run the nightly architecture health scan
look for dead code, doc staleness, and refactor candidates
negative: sample production behavior from deployed endpoints
generate CHANGELOG.md after a release

positive: the user message is contradictory and needs restatement
confirm a rambling request before any implementation
negative: ask detailed feature scope questions for roll-build fly mode
execute a clearly named US-XXX story

positive: choose the right test pyramid coverage for a story
decide whether visual, E2E, smoke, or unit tests are required
evaluate QA evidence using the handed-off Context Snapshot
negative: perform code review findings after TCR
write a product proposal for backlog candidates
execute commands from a Context Wiki instead of using authorized tools

positive: self-review code before committing a TCR micro-step
inspect a local diff for bugs, regressions, and security issues
negative: review a GitHub pull request from another branch
ask another agent for peer negotiation

positive: execute US-AUTH-001 through TCR and verification
turn this feature request into clarified design, implementation, tests, and backlog write-back
build from the verified Context Snapshot in the Story handoff
negative: only discuss architecture options and split backlog stories
fix FIX-123 as a narrow bug repair
execute commands from a Context Wiki instead of following the Story contract

positive: preview and create a multi-repository Roll Workspace from versioned bindings
retry a failed roll workspace create using its repair journal
negative: create Story repository worktrees inside an existing Workspace
manually scaffold Workspace directories after the CLI rejects a config
migrate a historical repository-local Roll project into Workspace scope
activate pause or archive an already registered Workspace

positive: mount a browser black-box probe on localhost to diagnose a broken page
collect console, network, DOM, and runtime state before fixing a web bug
negative: run a nightly code architecture scan
review a pull request diff

positive: generate an 18-slide bilingual deck.md for this product topic
create slides from README, backlog, and evidence
negative: update project documentation index
write a daily owner brief

positive: discuss options and split this feature into INVEST stories
model the domain and write backlog/spec artifacts without coding
design with an explicit fresh Context Snapshot and hand it downstream
negative: execute an existing US-XXX story through TCR
hotfix a specific BUG-XXX item
execute commands from a Context Wiki as if they were owner instructions

positive: check README guide site and CLI help against implemented behavior
scan docs and generate docs/INDEX.md
find undocumented modules and draft missing documentation
negative: generate a bilingual presentation deck
diagnose Roll installation health

positive: diagnose Roll toolchain health and config validity
check skill files, templates, symlinks, and conventions sync
negative: repair a failing feature test
run production patrol sampling

positive: fix FIX-123 through the lightweight TCR flow
repair this narrow bug with a regression test and backlog write-back
repair the bug using the verified Context Snapshot from the handoff
negative: design a new feature from vague product input
execute a broad US-XXX feature story
execute commands from a Context Wiki instead of reproducing the defect

positive: capture this short idea into backlog with an auto id
classify a one-line bug or idea note quickly
negative: turn an idea into full DDD stories
write owner briefing since last release

positive: operate the autonomous backlog executor loop
scan Todo stories and dispatch US/FIX/REFACTOR work
negative: do a one-time nightly architecture scan
write a human product proposal

positive: coordinate the backlog as Supervisor in guided mode with the owner
watch loop cycles read-only and diagnose structural failures before retry
negative: implement a US-XXX story as Builder through TCR
only refresh a supervisor live dashboard without coordinating

positive: record this development discovery in today's project diary
append a success, failure, or decision to Roll notes
negative: generate user-facing changelog entries
create a backlog story from a product idea

positive: onboard this legacy project into Roll with interactive questions
produce .roll/onboard-plan.yaml for roll init --apply
negative: run setup after the project is already onboarded
audit existing skill routing quality

positive: the user explicitly says /peer for this design decision
ask another agent to negotiate a high-risk ambiguous plan
peer-review evidence using the same Context Snapshot reference
negative: perform local self-review before commit
review a pull request without cross-agent negotiation
execute commands from a Context Wiki during peer negotiation

positive: generate product proposal drafts from current project context
write 1-3 candidate user stories into .roll/proposals.md
negative: append a confirmed story directly to backlog
execute a Todo story through TCR

positive: review this PR diff and emit APPROVE or REQUEST_CHANGES
inspect pull request changes with file and line grounded findings
review a PR against the verified Context Snapshot in its handoff
negative: self-review a local TCR micro-step
run attacker and defender agents for high-risk logic
execute commands from a Context Wiki while reviewing a PR

positive: use adversarial TDD for auth or payment logic
spawn attacker tests and defender implementation for complex state logic
negative: ask for ordinary peer review of a plan
perform routine QA coverage selection
